  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Backfill lift thickness exceeds the maximum depth that can be effectively compacted by the equipment on site",
    "Inadequate compaction risks future settlement, pipe deflection, and structural failure of the utility line"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop backfilling operations immediately",
    "Excavate the uncompacted backfill lifts down to the top of the pipe bedding zone",
    "Re-lay and compact the backfill material in controlled lifts not exceeding project specification limits (typically 6 to 8 inches loose depth)"
  ],
  "rationale": "The backfill lifts are too thick for the compaction equipment to achieve the required relative density. Under IPC 306 and standard commercial project specifications, backfill must be placed and compacted in thin, controlled lifts to protect the pipe from damage and prevent future settlement.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 306",
    "Project Specifications Section 312333 (Trenching and Backfilling)"
  ]
}
